visual_base.visual_narrative_log: (1) A young woman with long brown hair, wearing a grey long-sleeved shirt with small floral patterns, stands before a light-colored table. On the table sits a white plate with large, raw white onion slices and a cardboard box containing several steamed dumplings (xiaolongbao). She gestures excitedly with her hands near her face. (2) The woman picks up a small bottle of dark liquid (soy sauce) and pours it directly over the dumplings in the box. Yellow text overlays appear on the screen. (3) Using chopsticks, she picks up a piece of raw onion, then places a whole dumpling on top of it. She lifts this combination to her mouth, showing a wide-eyed, expressive look. (4) She eats the large bite in one go, chewing vigorously with noticeable facial expressions of enjoyment and surprise. (5) For the second 'bite', she picks up another dumpling, this time topped with a smaller piece of food and more sauce poured from a black bottle. She consumes it quickly. (6) For the third bite, she creates a stack consisting of two onion slices sandwiching a dumpling and a small round cracker. She pours more sauce on top and stuffs the entire large combination into her mouth. (7) After struggling to chew the massive third bite, she appears to start choking or experiencing discomfort, touching her throat. She then grabs a red can of Coca-Cola, opens it, and drinks it while still chewing, looking at the camera with a comical, wide-eyed expression.
visual_base.text_narrative_log: (1) [Subtitle]: 你們知道韓國現在流行. (2) [Slogan]: 三口減肥法. (3) [Subtitle]: 就是你想吃的東西任何東西都可以吃. [Subtitle]: 但只能吃三口. (4) [Subtitle]: 然後我準備的小籠包. (5) [Subtitle]: (小籠包). (6) [Subtitle]: 第一口. [Subtitle]: 先準備洋蔥!. (7) [Subtitle]: 果然配著洋蔥一起吃. (8) [Subtitle]: 整個口味變得超清爽耶. [Variety]: 有點辣. (9) [Subtitle]: 早就知道會這樣. [Subtitle]: 我再準備了... [Variety]: ???. (10) [Subtitle]: 說一套, 做一套. [Subtitle]: 加了火雞辣醬. (11) [Subtitle]: 第二口. [Subtitle]: 減肥太簡單了吧. [Subtitle]: 好好吃喔. (12) [Subtitle]: 第三口. [Subtitle]: 看起來有點像雪人. (13) [Subtitle]: 反正能一口吃掉. [Subtitle]: 不就好了嗎?. (14) [Variety]: 突然喉嚨被噎到. [Subtitle]: 真的沒辦法. (15) [Subtitle]: 只好喝了可樂. (16) [Subtitle]: 三口減肥法好簡單喔.

visual_human_presence.countingEvidence: 1 main female creator recording a mukbang video.
visual_human_presence.mainCharacterDescription: Young woman with long, straight brown hair, light makeup with bright red lipstick, wearing a grey-blue long-sleeved top with a subtle pattern.

visual_scene_and_style.backgroundDescribe: A plain, neutrally toned indoor wall, likely a bedroom or studio setup with a simple wooden-style table.

audio_base.audio_narrative_log: (1) A female voice speaks with high energy and a rising pitch, introducing a trendy 'three-bite weight loss method' in Traditional Chinese. A light, upbeat acoustic guitar-driven BGM with a soft female vocal begins. (2) A clear liquid-pouring sound effect is heard as she describes preparing her food. (3) Rhythmic chewing sounds (mukbang-style) are emphasized as she takes the first 'bite', punctuated by a comedic 'ding' sound effect. The BGM remains steady in the background. (4) The speaker continues her enthusiastic narration, interrupted by more crunching and swallowing sounds. (5) A sharp mechanical click and a popping sound occur as a soda can is opened. (6) Swallowing and gulping sounds are prominent as she drinks, followed by a final comedic comment delivered with a laughing, high-arousal vocal tone as the BGM fades.

narrative_and_purpose.narrativeStructure: List-based: Hook -> Concept Intro -> Sequential Execution -> Twist Ending
narrative_and_purpose.mainPurpose: To entertain the audience with a humorous 'life hack' or 'diet trend' parody. The creator uses the concept of 'only three bites' to justify eating extremely large, stacked portions of dumplings and onions, ending with a relatable moment of choking and needing a soda, thereby satirizing unrealistic weight loss trends.

endorsement_analysis.endorsementCues: Brand items (Coca-Cola) appear as part of the comedic narrative rather than as a primary focus for promotion.

high_inference.freeDescribe: The video blends Mukbang elements with social media comedy. The use of large white onions as a 'dietary' component is likely for visual contrast and comedic effect.
